chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1075 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.4462/2023 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at Flag ‘A’ 3. For orders on CMA No.4463/2023 (ExA) 4. For orders on CMA No.4464/2023 (S/A) 5. For hearing of main case 27.06.2023 Mr. Ghulam Akbar Dharejo, Advocate along with Petitioners <><><><>..<><><><> 1. Urgency is granted. 2to5. Counsel contends that earlier a Petition being C.P No.D- 961/2023 filed by the present petitioners, was dismissed for non-prosecution vide order dated 13.06.2023; however, he submits that the reason for non-attendance of the petitioners was that they were afraid. We are not very satisfied with the explanation, which has been provided. No arrest order was in the field and perhaps the petitioners thought it better to disappear thereafter, then had filed this petition and have appeared, rather than seek bail in the FIR, which has been filed. Yet, showing indulgence, the police is once again restrained from arresting the petitioners for a period of 10 days (in the case arising out of F.I.R No.02 of 2023 registered at the Jhali Kalwari police station) during which time, both the petitioners shall record their statements with the Investigating Officer and the Investigating Officer shall file the same before the learned Court seized of the matter along with supplementary challan. Counsel and both the petitioners have been cautioned that if the petitioners do not cooperate in recording their statements, no further indulgence or leniency will be shown to them. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed applications.

Counsel and both the petitioners have been cautioned that if the petitioners do not cooperate in recording their statements, no further indulgence or leniency will be shown to them. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed applications. Judge Judge ARBROHI
